ops.title: daemonkit v0.2 candidate API work from Wave 1/2 migration recon
ops.body:
Consumer recon during the migration waves (2026-07-18/19) reversed several planned adoptions because the extracted primitives are lossy generalizations of their donors. The gaps, recorded as v0.2 candidates:

**supervise (donor: cc-orchestrate's supervisor.go — which stays local until these exist):**
1. Pluggable/persisted budget + an external ResetStrikes(subject) hook — cc-orchestrate's RestartCount lives in SQLite, survives daemon restarts, and is reset by the transcript tailer on proven-healthy recovery; proc.Strikes is in-memory and only decays by time.
2. A confirmed-dead-without-live-probe mode — enumerable backends treat absence from ListAgents as authoritative death (no pid to probe); supervise mandates Stale→Alive corroboration.
3. A push entry point beside the pull tick — cc-orchestrate's childExited socket report drives respawn under the same budget/lock outside the ticker.
4. Per-subject StartupGrace override (minor).

**drain (donor gap, from Track FD recon):** the fusekit holder lifecycle (skew-triggered self-retire + LaunchAgent relaunch + mount-spec journal) needs the Track FD design before drain gains its second consumer; see fusekit cc-notes task 32df2a6.

**wire.Server:** cc-interact could not adopt it — the Response{version,payload} wrapper is incompatible with a consumer-frozen flat reply shape. The sub-primitives (Framing, PeerFromConn) carried the value instead. A v0.2 consideration: let Server serve consumer-owned envelope shapes, or document the sub-primitive path as the frozen-wire adoption route.

**KeyedMutex:** returns sync.Locker; consumers whose tests assert lock-held via TryLock cannot swap without fragile assertions — consider exposing *sync.Mutex or a TryLock-capable interface.
